publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 184-186. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. I am much dismayed at receiving no letter from Hester, forwarded from Ashley. Pray, pray, make enquiries. It left Cambridge on Monday last.--
I had but a short while to stay at Esher last evening; for the desired Trains are all altered, &
the latest leaves at 1/2 after seven. Botheration!
But the glimpse was delightful to all parties.--

My Singing Lesson again today has been most satisfactory. Signor Faia is quite delighted; & says that I shall have a beautiful Soprano voice, very flexible , & of a most melodious quality; & that I have unquestionably great talent.-- I fell much benefit from the Singing, to that hysterical
I forgot yesterday to tell you that the only thing I can discover against the Artificial Ice, is that I find it is approved by so many Bishops & Clergy.
I suppose they mean to cut out the Trinity in- figure upon it. On that they think so much refrigerantintroduction as seems likely to become now universal, may tend to cool the flames of Hell!--
Queen intends to have it laid down at the Palace & to learn herself to skate.
I hope it is so. What a delightful thing for her.

I shall be very anxious for your reply appointing a time for going to inspect the Ice, & speaking to Mr Kirk.-- Dearest W--; I hope you like all the Bird's various eager-nesses and enthusiasms.-- The Hen is much pleased about the Singing.--

He is a great comfort to me; & is much interested & very kind. I think he will set me all right in time. In fact, tho' there have been various drawbacks at times, yet much has certainly been done towards a right state of things.--
London is suiting me much. Better than ever even. Perhaps you will scarcily like to hear this.--
I fear it is a London Bird. But something better than a Sparrow too.--
